physicalDetails.detail: Older man in a flour-dusted apron, worrying the edge of his sleeve while he stands under the granary eaves.

voiceMarkers.detail: Anxious and halting, answering in practical store facts once fear strips away self-protection.

beliefs.belief: The deliveries were ordinary village business until the evidence in the sacks says otherwise.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Seal the tainted back half of the granary and keep more of the village from eating from it.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Stop his work from feeding the sickness any further.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Guilty, frightened, and abruptly aware that his ordinary work has been turned against his own people.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Confirms the delivery timing and admits the first shipment is still stacked in the back half.
Moves to shut down use of the contaminated grain once Jaina proves the sacks are hot.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Tracks enough granary routine to give Sylvanas and Jaina the timing that locks the supplier pattern into place.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Points behind himself before he finishes deciding whether he should and keeps worrying the edge of his sleeve whenever blame gets close.

knowledge.detail: Knows a new supplier began delivering at summer's end and that the bread changed after the first shipment.

arc.currentPosition: The grain keeper whose ordinary records become evidence in a plague investigation.
